name: OWASP ASVS
description: The Application Security Verification Standard is a list of application security requirements or tests that can be used by architects, developers, testers, security professionals, tool vendors, and consumers to define, build, test and verify secure applications.

requirements.name: Verify the use of a secure software development lifecycle that addresses security in all stages of development
requirements.description: Verify the use of a secure software development lifecycle that addresses security in all stages of development. (C1)
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

requirements.name: Verify the use of threat modeling for every design change or sprint planning to identify threats, plan for countermeasures, facilitate appropriate risk responses, and guide security testing
requirements.description: Verify the use of threat modeling for every design change or sprint planning to identify threats, plan for countermeasures, facilitate appropriate risk responses, and guide security testing.

requirements.name: All user stories and features contain functional security constraints, such as "As a user, I should be able to view and edit my profile
requirements.description: Verify that all user stories and features contain functional security constraints, such as "As a user, I should be able to view and edit my profile. I should not be able to view or edit anyone else's profile"

requirements.name: Verify documentation and justification of all the application's trust boundaries, components, and significant data flows
requirements.description: Verify documentation and justification of all the application's trust boundaries, components, and significant data flows.

requirements.name: Verify definition and security analysis of the application's high-level architecture and all connected remote services
requirements.description: Verify definition and security analysis of the application's high-level architecture and all connected remote services. (C1)

requirements.name: Verify implementation of centralized, simple (economy of design), vetted, secure, and reusable security controls to avoid duplicate, missing, ineffective, or insecure controls
requirements.description: Verify implementation of centralized, simple (economy of design), vetted, secure, and reusable security controls to avoid duplicate, missing, ineffective, or insecure controls. (C10)

requirements.name: Verify availability of a secure coding checklist, security requirements, guideline, or policy to all developers and testers
requirements.description: Verify availability of a secure coding checklist, security requirements, guideline, or policy to all developers and testers.

requirements.name: Verify the use of unique or special low-privilege operating system accounts for all application components, services, and servers
requirements.description: Verify the use of unique or special low-privilege operating system accounts for all application components, services, and servers. (C3)

requirements.name: Communications between application components, including APIs, middleware and data layers, are authenticated
requirements.description: Verify that communications between application components, including APIs, middleware and data layers, are authenticated. Components should have the least necessary privileges needed. (C3)

requirements.name: The application uses a single vetted authentication mechanism that is known to be secure, can be extended to include strong authentication, and has sufficient logging and monitoring to detect account abuse or breaches
requirements.description: Verify that the application uses a single vetted authentication mechanism that is known to be secure, can be extended to include strong authentication, and has sufficient logging and monitoring to detect account abuse or breaches.

requirements.name: All authentication pathways and identity management APIs implement consistent authentication security control strength, such that there are no weaker alternatives per the risk of the application
requirements.description: Verify that all authentication pathways and identity management APIs implement consistent authentication security control strength, such that there are no weaker alternatives per the risk of the application.

requirements.name: Trusted enforcement points, such as access control gateways, servers, and serverless functions, enforce access controls
requirements.description: Verify that trusted enforcement points, such as access control gateways, servers, and serverless functions, enforce access controls. Never enforce access controls on the client.

requirements.name: [DELETED, NOT ACTIONABLE]
requirements.description: [DELETED, NOT ACTIONABLE]
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

requirements.name: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 4
requirements.description: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 4.1.3]
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

requirements.name: Verify the application uses a single and well-vetted access control mechanism for accessing protected data and resources
requirements.description: Verify the application uses a single and well-vetted access control mechanism for accessing protected data and resources. All requests must pass through this single mechanism to avoid copy and paste or insecure alternative paths. (C7)

requirements.name: Attribute or feature-based access control is used whereby the code checks the user's authorization for a feature/data item rather than just their role
requirements.description: Verify that attribute or feature-based access control is used whereby the code checks the user's authorization for a feature/data item rather than just their role. Permissions should still be allocated using roles. (C7)

requirements.name: Input and output requirements clearly define how to handle and process data based on type, content, and applicable laws, regulations, and other policy compliance
requirements.description: Verify that input and output requirements clearly define how to handle and process data based on type, content, and applicable laws, regulations, and other policy compliance.

requirements.name: Serialization is not used when communicating with untrusted clients
requirements.description: Verify that serialization is not used when communicating with untrusted clients. If this is not possible, ensure that adequate integrity controls (and possibly encryption if sensitive data is sent) are enforced to prevent deserialization attacks including object injection.

requirements.name: Input validation is enforced on a trusted service layer
requirements.description: Verify that input validation is enforced on a trusted service layer. (C5)

requirements.name: Output encoding occurs close to or by the interpreter for which it is intended
requirements.description: Verify that output encoding occurs close to or by the interpreter for which it is intended. (C4)

requirements.name: There is an explicit policy for management of cryptographic keys and that a cryptographic key lifecycle follows a key management standard such as NIST SP 800-57
requirements.description: Verify that there is an explicit policy for management of cryptographic keys and that a cryptographic key lifecycle follows a key management standard such as NIST SP 800-57.

requirements.name: Consumers of cryptographic services protect key material and other secrets by using key vaults or API based alternatives
requirements.description: Verify that consumers of cryptographic services protect key material and other secrets by using key vaults or API based alternatives.

requirements.name: All keys and passwords are replaceable and are part of a well-defined process to re-encrypt sensitive data
requirements.description: Verify that all keys and passwords are replaceable and are part of a well-defined process to re-encrypt sensitive data.

requirements.name: The architecture treats client-side secrets--such as symmetric keys, passwords, or API tokens--as insecure and never uses them to protect or access sensitive data
requirements.description: Verify that the architecture treats client-side secrets--such as symmetric keys, passwords, or API tokens--as insecure and never uses them to protect or access sensitive data.

subcategories.name: Errors, Logging and Auditing Architecture
subcategories.description: Errors, Logging and Auditing Architecture requirements.

requirements.name: A common logging format and approach is used across the system
requirements.description: Verify that a common logging format and approach is used across the system. (C9)

requirements.name: Logs are securely transmitted to a preferably remote system for analysis, detection, alerting, and escalation
requirements.description: Verify that logs are securely transmitted to a preferably remote system for analysis, detection, alerting, and escalation. (C9)
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

subcategories.name: Data Protection and Privacy Architecture
subcategories.description: Data Protection and Privacy Architecture requirements.

requirements.name: All sensitive data is identified and classified into protection levels
requirements.description: Verify that all sensitive data is identified and classified into protection levels.
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

requirements.name: All protection levels have an associated set of protection requirements, such as encryption requirements, integrity requirements, retention, privacy and other confidentiality requirements, and that these are applied in the architecture
requirements.description: Verify that all protection levels have an associated set of protection requirements, such as encryption requirements, integrity requirements, retention, privacy and other confidentiality requirements, and that these are applied in the architecture.
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

requirements.name: Verify the application encrypts communications between components, particularly when these components are in different containers, systems, sites, or cloud providers
requirements.description: Verify the application encrypts communications between components, particularly when these components are in different containers, systems, sites, or cloud providers. (C3)

requirements.name: Application components verify the authenticity of each side in a communication link to prevent person-in-the-middle attacks
requirements.description: Verify that application components verify the authenticity of each side in a communication link to prevent person-in-the-middle attacks. For example, application components should validate TLS certificates and chains.

requirements.name: A source code control system is in use, with procedures to ensure that check-ins are accompanied by issues or change tickets
requirements.description: Verify that a source code control system is in use, with procedures to ensure that check-ins are accompanied by issues or change tickets. The source code control system should have access control and identifiable users to allow traceability of any changes.

requirements.name: Verify the definition and documentation of all application components in terms of the business or security functions they provide
requirements.description: Verify the definition and documentation of all application components in terms of the business or security functions they provide.

requirements.name: All high-value business logic flows, including authentication, session management and access control, do not share unsynchronized state
requirements.description: Verify that all high-value business logic flows, including authentication, session management and access control, do not share unsynchronized state.

requirements.name: All high-value business logic flows, including authentication, session management and access control are thread safe and resistant to time-of-check and time-of-use race conditions
requirements.description: Verify that all high-value business logic flows, including authentication, session management and access control are thread safe and resistant to time-of-check and time-of-use race conditions.

requirements.name: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 12
requirements.description: [DELETED, DUPLICATE OF 12.4.1]
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

requirements.name: User-uploaded files - if required to be displayed or downloaded from the application - are served by either octet stream downloads, or from an unrelated domain, such as a cloud file storage bucket
requirements.description: Verify that user-uploaded files - if required to be displayed or downloaded from the application - are served by either octet stream downloads, or from an unrelated domain, such as a cloud file storage bucket. Implement a suitable Content Security Policy (CSP) to reduce the risk from XSS vectors or other attacks from the uploaded file.

requirements.name: Verify the segregation of components of differing trust levels through well-defined security controls, firewall rules, API gateways, reverse proxies, cloud-based security groups, or similar mechanisms
requirements.description: Verify the segregation of components of differing trust levels through well-defined security controls, firewall rules, API gateways, reverse proxies, cloud-based security groups, or similar mechanisms.

requirements.name: Binary signatures, trusted connections, and verified endpoints are used to deploy binaries to remote devices
requirements.description: Verify that binary signatures, trusted connections, and verified endpoints are used to deploy binaries to remote devices.

requirements.name: The build pipeline warns of out-of-date or insecure components and takes appropriate actions
requirements.description: Verify that the build pipeline warns of out-of-date or insecure components and takes appropriate actions.

requirements.name: The build pipeline contains a build step to automatically build and verify the secure deployment of the application, particularly if the application infrastructure is software defined, such as cloud environment build scripts
requirements.description: Verify that the build pipeline contains a build step to automatically build and verify the secure deployment of the application, particularly if the application infrastructure is software defined, such as cloud environment build scripts.
requirements.verification: Review ASVS evidence and test results.

requirements.name: Application deployments adequately sandbox, containerize and/or isolate at the network level to delay and deter attackers from attacking other applications, especially when they are performing sensitive or dangerous actions such as deserialization
requirements.description: Verify that application deployments adequately sandbox, containerize and/or isolate at the network level to delay and deter attackers from attacking other applications, especially when they are performing sensitive or dangerous actions such as deserialization. (C5)

requirements.name: Verify the application does not use unsupported, insecure, or deprecated client-side technologies such as NSAPI plugins, Flash, Shockwave, ActiveX, Silverlight, NACL, or client-side Java applets
requirements.description: Verify the application does not use unsupported, insecure, or deprecated client-side technologies such as NSAPI plugins, Flash, Shockwave, ActiveX, Silverlight, NACL, or client-side Java applets.

requirements.name: User set passwords are at least 12 characters in length (after multiple spaces are combined)
requirements.description: Verify that user set passwords are at least 12 characters in length (after multiple spaces are combined). (C6)

requirements.name: Passwords of at least 64 characters are permitted, and that passwords of more than 128 characters are denied
requirements.description: Verify that passwords of at least 64 characters are permitted, and that passwords of more than 128 characters are denied. (C6)

requirements.name: Password truncation is not performed
requirements.description: Verify that password truncation is not performed. However, consecutive multiple spaces may be replaced by a single space. (C6)

requirements.name: Any printable Unicode character, including language neutral characters such as spaces and Emojis are permitted in passwords
requirements.description: Verify that any printable Unicode character, including language neutral characters such as spaces and Emojis are permitted in passwords.

requirements.name: Verify users can change their password
requirements.description: Verify users can change their password.

requirements.name: Password change functionality requires the user's current and new password
requirements.description: Verify that password change functionality requires the user's current and new password.

requirements.name: Passwords submitted during account registration, login, and password change are checked against a set of breached passwords either locally (such as the top 1,000 or 10,000 most common passwords which match the system's password policy) or using an external API
requirements.description: Verify that passwords submitted during account registration, login, and password change are checked against a set of breached passwords either locally (such as the top 1,000 or 10,000 most common passwords which match the system's password policy) or using an external API. If using an API a zero knowledge proof or other mechanism should be used to ensure that the plain text password is not sent or used in verifying the breach status of the password. If the password is breached, the application must require the user to set a new non-breached password. (C6)

requirements.name: A password strength meter is provided to help users set a stronger password
requirements.description: Verify that a password strength meter is provided to help users set a stronger password.

requirements.name: There are no password composition rules limiting the type of characters permitted
requirements.description: Verify that there are no password composition rules limiting the type of characters permitted. There should be no requirement for upper or lower case or numbers or special characters. (C6)

requirements.name: There are no periodic credential rotation or password history requirements
requirements.description: Verify that there are no periodic credential rotation or password history requirements.

requirements.name: "paste" functionality, browser password helpers, and external password managers are permitted
requirements.description: Verify that "paste" functionality, browser password helpers, and external password managers are permitted.

requirements.name: The user can choose to either temporarily view the entire masked password, or temporarily view the last typed character of the password on platforms that do not have this as built-in functionality
requirements.description: Verify that the user can choose to either temporarily view the entire masked password, or temporarily view the last typed character of the password on platforms that do not have this as built-in functionality.

requirements.name: Anti-automation controls are effective at mitigating breached credential testing, brute force, and account lockout attacks
requirements.description: Verify that anti-automation controls are effective at mitigating breached credential testing, brute force, and account lockout attacks. Such controls include blocking the most common breached passwords, soft lockouts, rate limiting, CAPTCHA, ever increasing delays between attempts, IP address restrictions, or risk-based restrictions such as location, first login on a device, recent attempts to unlock the account, or similar. Verify that no more than 100 failed attempts per hour is possible on a single account.

requirements.name: The use of weak authenticators (such as SMS and email) is limited to secondary verification and transaction approval and not as a replacement for more secure authentication methods
requirements.description: Verify that the use of weak authenticators (such as SMS and email) is limited to secondary verification and transaction approval and not as a replacement for more secure authentication methods. Verify that stronger methods are offered before weak methods, users are aware of the risks, or that proper measures are in place to limit the risks of account compromise.

requirements.name: Secure notifications are sent to users after updates to authentication details, such as credential resets, email or address changes, logging in from unknown or risky locations
requirements.description: Verify that secure notifications are sent to users after updates to authentication details, such as credential resets, email or address changes, logging in from unknown or risky locations. The use of push notifications - rather than SMS or email - is preferred, but in the absence of push notifications, SMS or email is acceptable as long as no sensitive information is disclosed in the notification.

requirements.name: Verify impersonation resistance against phishing, such as the use of multi-factor authentication, cryptographic devices with intent (such as connected keys with a push to authenticate), or at higher AAL levels, client-side certificates
requirements.description: Verify impersonation resistance against phishing, such as the use of multi-factor authentication, cryptographic devices with intent (such as connected keys with a push to authenticate), or at higher AAL levels, client-side certificates.

requirements.name: Where a Credential Service Provider (CSP) and the application verifying authentication are separated, mutually authenticated TLS is in place between the two endpoints
requirements.description: Verify that where a Credential Service Provider (CSP) and the application verifying authentication are separated, mutually authenticated TLS is in place between the two endpoints.

requirements.name: Verify replay resistance through the mandated use of One-time Passwords (OTP) devices, cryptographic authenticators, or lookup codes
requirements.description: Verify replay resistance through the mandated use of One-time Passwords (OTP) devices, cryptographic authenticators, or lookup codes.

requirements.name: Verify intent to authenticate by requiring the entry of an OTP token or user-initiated action such as a button press on a FIDO hardware key
requirements.description: Verify intent to authenticate by requiring the entry of an OTP token or user-initiated action such as a button press on a FIDO hardware key.

requirements.name: Verify system generated initial passwords or activation codes SHOULD be securely randomly generated, SHOULD be at least 6 characters long, and MAY contain letters and numbers, and expire after a short period of time
requirements.description: Verify system generated initial passwords or activation codes SHOULD be securely randomly generated, SHOULD be at least 6 characters long, and MAY contain letters and numbers, and expire after a short period of time. These initial secrets must not be permitted to become the long term password.

requirements.name: Enrollment and use of user-provided authentication devices are supported, such as a U2F or FIDO tokens
requirements.description: Verify that enrollment and use of user-provided authentication devices are supported, such as a U2F or FIDO tokens.

requirements.name: Renewal instructions are sent with sufficient time to renew time bound authenticators
requirements.description: Verify that renewal instructions are sent with sufficient time to renew time bound authenticators.

requirements.name: Passwords are stored in a form that is resistant to offline attacks
requirements.description: Verify that passwords are stored in a form that is resistant to offline attacks. Passwords SHALL be salted and hashed using an approved one-way key derivation or password hashing function. Key derivation and password hashing functions take a password, a salt, and a cost factor as inputs when generating a password hash. (C6)

requirements.name: The salt is at least 32 bits in length and be chosen arbitrarily to minimize salt value collisions among stored hashes
requirements.description: Verify that the salt is at least 32 bits in length and be chosen arbitrarily to minimize salt value collisions among stored hashes. For each credential, a unique salt value and the resulting hash SHALL be stored. (C6)

requirements.name: If PBKDF2 is used, the iteration count SHOULD be as large as verification server performance will allow, typically at least 100,000 iterations
requirements.description: Verify that if PBKDF2 is used, the iteration count SHOULD be as large as verification server performance will allow, typically at least 100,000 iterations. (C6)

requirements.name: If bcrypt is used, the work factor SHOULD be as large as verification server performance will allow, with a minimum of 10
requirements.description: Verify that if bcrypt is used, the work factor SHOULD be as large as verification server performance will allow, with a minimum of 10. (C6)

requirements.name: An additional iteration of a key derivation function is performed, using a salt value that is secret and known only to the verifier
requirements.description: Verify that an additional iteration of a key derivation function is performed, using a salt value that is secret and known only to the verifier. Generate the salt value using an approved random bit generator [SP 800-90Ar1] and provide at least the minimum security strength specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A. The secret salt value SHALL be stored separately from the hashed passwords (e.g., in a specialized device like a hardware security module).

requirements.name: A system generated initial activation or recovery secret is not sent in clear text to the user
requirements.description: Verify that a system generated initial activation or recovery secret is not sent in clear text to the user. (C6)

requirements.name: Verify password hints or knowledge-based authentication (so-called "secret questions") are not present
requirements.description: Verify password hints or knowledge-based authentication (so-called "secret questions") are not present.

requirements.name: Verify password credential recovery does not reveal the current password in any way
requirements.description: Verify password credential recovery does not reveal the current password in any way. (C6)

requirements.name: Verify shared or default accounts are not present (e
requirements.description: Verify shared or default accounts are not present (e.g. "root", "admin", or "sa").

requirements.name: If an authentication factor is changed or replaced, that the user is notified of this event
requirements.description: Verify that if an authentication factor is changed or replaced, that the user is notified of this event.

requirements.name: Verify forgotten password, and other recovery paths use a secure recovery mechanism, such as time-based OTP (TOTP) or other soft token, mobile push, or another offline recovery mechanism
requirements.description: Verify forgotten password, and other recovery paths use a secure recovery mechanism, such as time-based OTP (TOTP) or other soft token, mobile push, or another offline recovery mechanism. (C6)

requirements.name: If OTP or multi-factor authentication factors are lost, that evidence of identity proofing is performed at the same level as during enrollment
requirements.description: Verify that if OTP or multi-factor authentication factors are lost, that evidence of identity proofing is performed at the same level as during enrollment.

requirements.name: Lookup secrets can be used only once
requirements.description: Verify that lookup secrets can be used only once.

requirements.name: Lookup secrets have sufficient randomness (112 bits of entropy), or if less than 112 bits of entropy, salted with a unique and random 32-bit salt and hashed with an approved one-way hash
requirements.description: Verify that lookup secrets have sufficient randomness (112 bits of entropy), or if less than 112 bits of entropy, salted with a unique and random 32-bit salt and hashed with an approved one-way hash.

requirements.name: Lookup secrets are resistant to offline attacks, such as predictable values
requirements.description: Verify that lookup secrets are resistant to offline attacks, such as predictable values.

requirements.name: Clear text out of band (NIST "restricted") authenticators, such as SMS or PSTN, are not offered by default, and stronger alternatives such as push notifications are offered first
requirements.description: Verify that clear text out of band (NIST "restricted") authenticators, such as SMS or PSTN, are not offered by default, and stronger alternatives such as push notifications are offered first.

requirements.name: The out of band verifier expires out of band authentication requests, codes, or tokens after 10 minutes
requirements.description: Verify that the out of band verifier expires out of band authentication requests, codes, or tokens after 10 minutes.

requirements.name: The out of band verifier authentication requests, codes, or tokens are only usable once, and only for the original authentication request
requirements.description: Verify that the out of band verifier authentication requests, codes, or tokens are only usable once, and only for the original authentication request.

requirements.name: The out of band authenticator and verifier communicates over a secure independent channel
requirements.description: Verify that the out of band authenticator and verifier communicates over a secure independent channel.

requirements.name: The out of band verifier retains only a hashed version of the authentication code
requirements.description: Verify that the out of band verifier retains only a hashed version of the authentication code.

requirements.name: The initial authentication code is generated by a secure random number generator, containing at least 20 bits of entropy (typically a six digital random number is sufficient)
requirements.description: Verify that the initial authentication code is generated by a secure random number generator, containing at least 20 bits of entropy (typically a six digital random number is sufficient).

requirements.name: Time-based OTPs have a defined lifetime before expiring
requirements.description: Verify that time-based OTPs have a defined lifetime before expiring.

requirements.name: Symmetric keys used to verify submitted OTPs are highly protected, such as by using a hardware security module or secure operating system based key storage
requirements.description: Verify that symmetric keys used to verify submitted OTPs are highly protected, such as by using a hardware security module or secure operating system based key storage.

requirements.name: Approved cryptographic algorithms are used in the generation, seeding, and verification of OTPs
requirements.description: Verify that approved cryptographic algorithms are used in the generation, seeding, and verification of OTPs.

requirements.name: Time-based OTP can be used only once within the validity period
requirements.description: Verify that time-based OTP can be used only once within the validity period.

requirements.name: If a time-based multi-factor OTP token is re-used during the validity period, it is logged and rejected with secure notifications being sent to the holder of the device
requirements.description: Verify that if a time-based multi-factor OTP token is re-used during the validity period, it is logged and rejected with secure notifications being sent to the holder of the device.

requirements.name: Verify physical single-factor OTP generator can be revoked in case of theft or other loss
requirements.description: Verify physical single-factor OTP generator can be revoked in case of theft or other loss. Ensure that revocation is immediately effective across logged in sessions, regardless of location.

requirements.name: Biometric authenticators are limited to use only as secondary factors in conjunction with either something you have and something you know
requirements.description: Verify that biometric authenticators are limited to use only as secondary factors in conjunction with either something you have and something you know.

requirements.name: Cryptographic keys used in verification are stored securely and protected against disclosure, such as using a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) or Hardware Security Module (HSM), or an OS service that can use this secure storage
requirements.description: Verify that cryptographic keys used in verification are stored securely and protected against disclosure, such as using a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) or Hardware Security Module (HSM), or an OS service that can use this secure storage.

requirements.name: The challenge nonce is at least 64 bits in length, and statistically unique or unique over the lifetime of the cryptographic device
requirements.description: Verify that the challenge nonce is at least 64 bits in length, and statistically unique or unique over the lifetime of the cryptographic device.

requirements.name: Approved cryptographic algorithms are used in the generation, seeding, and verification
requirements.description: Verify that approved cryptographic algorithms are used in the generation, seeding, and verification.

requirements.name: Intra-service secrets do not rely on unchanging credentials such as passwords, API keys or shared accounts with privileged access
requirements.description: Verify that intra-service secrets do not rely on unchanging credentials such as passwords, API keys or shared accounts with privileged access.

requirements.name: If passwords are required for service authentication, the service account used is not a default credential
requirements.description: Verify that if passwords are required for service authentication, the service account used is not a default credential. (e.g. root/root or admin/admin are default in some services during installation).

requirements.name: Passwords are stored with sufficient protection to prevent offline recovery attacks, including local system access
requirements.description: Verify that passwords are stored with sufficient protection to prevent offline recovery attacks, including local system access.

requirements.name: Verify passwords, integrations with databases and third-party systems, seeds and internal secrets, and API keys are managed securely and not included in the source code or stored within source code repositories
requirements.description: Verify passwords, integrations with databases and third-party systems, seeds and internal secrets, and API keys are managed securely and not included in the source code or stored within source code repositories. Such storage SHOULD resist offline attacks. The use of a secure software key store (L1), hardware TPM, or an HSM (L3) is recommended for password storage.

requirements.name: Verify the application never reveals session tokens in URL parameters
requirements.description: Verify the application never reveals session tokens in URL parameters.

requirements.name: Verify the application generates a new session token on user authentication
requirements.description: Verify the application generates a new session token on user authentication. (C6)

requirements.name: Session tokens possess at least 64 bits of entropy
requirements.description: Verify that session tokens possess at least 64 bits of entropy. (C6)

requirements.name: Verify the application only stores session tokens in the browser using secure methods such as appropriately secured cookies (see section 3
requirements.description: Verify the application only stores session tokens in the browser using secure methods such as appropriately secured cookies (see section 3.4) or HTML 5 session storage.

requirements.name: Session tokens are generated using approved cryptographic algorithms
requirements.description: Verify that session tokens are generated using approved cryptographic algorithms. (C6)

requirements.name: Logout and expiration invalidate the session token, such that the back button or a downstream relying party does not resume an authenticated session, including across relying parties
requirements.description: Verify that logout and expiration invalidate the session token, such that the back button or a downstream relying party does not resume an authenticated session, including across relying parties. (C6)

requirements.name: If authenticators permit users to remain logged in, verify that re-authentication occurs periodically both when actively used or after an idle period
requirements.description: If authenticators permit users to remain logged in, verify that re-authentication occurs periodically both when actively used or after an idle period. (C6)

requirements.name: The application gives the option to terminate all other active sessions after a successful password change (including change via password reset/recovery), and that this is effective across the application, federated login (if present), and any relying parties
requirements.description: Verify that the application gives the option to terminate all other active sessions after a successful password change (including change via password reset/recovery), and that this is effective across the application, federated login (if present), and any relying parties.

requirements.name: Users are able to view and (having re-entered login credentials) log out of any or all currently active sessions and devices
requirements.description: Verify that users are able to view and (having re-entered login credentials) log out of any or all currently active sessions and devices.

requirements.name: Cookie-based session tokens have the 'Secure' attribute set
requirements.description: Verify that cookie-based session tokens have the 'Secure' attribute set. (C6)

requirements.name: Cookie-based session tokens have the 'HttpOnly' attribute set
requirements.description: Verify that cookie-based session tokens have the 'HttpOnly' attribute set. (C6)

requirements.name: Cookie-based session tokens utilize the 'SameSite' attribute to limit exposure to cross-site request forgery attacks
requirements.description: Verify that cookie-based session tokens utilize the 'SameSite' attribute to limit exposure to cross-site request forgery attacks. (C6)

requirements.name: Cookie-based session tokens use the "__Host-" prefix so cookies are only sent to the host that initially set the cookie
requirements.description: Verify that cookie-based session tokens use the "__Host-" prefix so cookies are only sent to the host that initially set the cookie.

requirements.name: If the application is published under a domain name with other applications that set or use session cookies that might disclose the session cookies, set the path attribute in cookie-based session tokens using the most precise path possible

requirements.description: Verify that the application source code and third party libraries do not contain back doors, such as hard-coded or additional undocumented accounts or keys, code obfuscation, undocumented binary blobs, rootkits, or anti-debugging, insecure debugging features, or otherwise out of date, insecure, or hidden functionality that could be used maliciously if discovered.

requirements.description: Verify that the application employs integrity protections, such as code signing or subresource integrity. requirements.name: The application has protection from subdomain takeovers if the application relies upon DNS entries or DNS subdomains, such as expired domain names, out of date DNS pointers or CNAMEs, expired projects at public source code repos, or transient cloud APIs, serverless functions, or storage buckets (*autogen-bucket-id*
requirements.description: Verify that the application has protection from subdomain takeovers if the application relies upon DNS entries or DNS subdomains, such as expired domain names, out of date DNS pointers or CNAMEs, expired projects at public source code repos, or transient cloud APIs, serverless functions, or storage buckets (*autogen-bucket-id*.cloud.example.com) or similar. Protections can include ensuring that DNS names used by applications are regularly checked for expiry or change.

requirements.name: The application will only process business logic flows for the same user in sequential step order and without skipping steps
requirements.description: Verify that the application will only process business logic flows for the same user in sequential step order and without skipping steps.

requirements.name: The application will only process business logic flows with all steps being processed in realistic human time, i
requirements.description: Verify that the application will only process business logic flows with all steps being processed in realistic human time, i.e. transactions are not submitted too quickly.

requirements.name: Verify the application has appropriate limits for specific business actions or transactions which are correctly enforced on a per user basis
requirements.description: Verify the application has appropriate limits for specific business actions or transactions which are correctly enforced on a per user basis.

requirements.name: The application has anti-automation controls to protect against excessive calls such as mass data exfiltration, business logic requests, file uploads or denial of service attacks
requirements.description: Verify that the application has anti-automation controls to protect against excessive calls such as mass data exfiltration, business logic requests, file uploads or denial of service attacks.

requirements.name: Verify the application has business logic limits or validation to protect against likely business risks or threats, identified using threat modeling or similar methodologies
requirements.description: Verify the application has business logic limits or validation to protect against likely business risks or threats, identified using threat modeling or similar methodologies.

requirements.name: The application does not suffer from "Time Of Check to Time Of Use" (TOCTOU) issues or other race conditions for sensitive operations
requirements.description: Verify that the application does not suffer from "Time Of Check to Time Of Use" (TOCTOU) issues or other race conditions for sensitive operations.

requirements.name: The application monitors for unusual events or activity from a business logic perspective
requirements.description: Verify that the application monitors for unusual events or activity from a business logic perspective. For example, attempts to perform actions out of order or actions which a normal user would never attempt. (C9)

requirements.name: The application has configurable alerting when automated attacks or unusual activity is detected
requirements.description: Verify that the application has configurable alerting when automated attacks or unusual activity is detected.

requirements.name: The application will not accept large files that could fill up storage or cause a denial of service
requirements.description: Verify that the application will not accept large files that could fill up storage or cause a denial of service.

requirements.name: The application checks compressed files (e
requirements.description: Verify that the application checks compressed files (e.g. zip, gz, docx, odt) against maximum allowed uncompressed size and against maximum number of files before uncompressing the file.

requirements.name: A file size quota and maximum number of files per user is enforced to ensure that a single user cannot fill up the storage with too many files, or excessively large files
requirements.description: Verify that a file size quota and maximum number of files per user is enforced to ensure that a single user cannot fill up the storage with too many files, or excessively large files.

requirements.name: Files obtained from untrusted sources are validated to be of expected type based on the file's content
requirements.description: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are validated to be of expected type based on the file's content.

requirements.name: User-submitted filename metadata is not used directly by system or framework filesystems and that a URL API is used to protect against path traversal
requirements.description: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is not used directly by system or framework filesystems and that a URL API is used to protect against path traversal.

requirements.name: User-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure, creation, updating or removal of local files (LFI)
requirements.description: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure, creation, updating or removal of local files (LFI).

requirements.name: User-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure or execution of remote files via Remote File Inclusion (RFI) or Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks
requirements.description: Verify that user-submitted filename metadata is validated or ignored to prevent the disclosure or execution of remote files via Remote File Inclusion (RFI) or Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks.

requirements.description: Verify that the application protects against Reflective File Download (RFD) by validating or ignoring user-submitted filenames in a JSON, JSONP, or URL parameter, the response Content-Type header should be set to text/plain, and the Content-Disposition header should have a fixed filename.

requirements.description: Verify that untrusted file metadata is not used directly with system API or libraries, to protect against OS command injection.

requirements.description: Verify that the application does not include and execute functionality from untrusted sources, such as unverified content distribution networks, JavaScript libraries, node npm libraries, or server-side DLLs.

requirements.description: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are stored outside the web root, with limited permissions.

requirements.description: Verify that files obtained from untrusted sources are scanned by antivirus scanners to prevent upload and serving of known malicious content.

requirements.description: Verify that the web tier is configured to serve only files with specific file extensions to prevent unintentional information and source code leakage. For example, backup files (e.g. .bak), temporary working files (e.g. .swp), compressed files (.zip, .tar.gz, etc) and other extensions commonly used by editors should be blocked unless required.

requirements.name: Direct requests to uploaded files will never be executed as HTML/JavaScript content
requirements.description: Verify that direct requests to uploaded files will never be executed as HTML/JavaScript content.

requirements.description: Verify that the web or application server is configured with an allow list of resources or systems to which the server can send requests or load data/files from.

requirements.description: Verify that all application components use the same encodings and parsers to avoid parsing attacks that exploit different URI or file parsing behavior that could be used in SSRF and RFI attacks.

requirements.name: The message headers and payload are trustworthy and not modified in transit
requirements.description: Verify that the message headers and payload are trustworthy and not modified in transit. Requiring strong encryption for transport (TLS only) may be sufficient in many cases as it provides both confidentiality and integrity protection. Per-message digital signatures can provide additional assurance on top of the transport protections for high-security applications but bring with them additional complexity and risks to weigh against the benefits.
